skill.name: enact-text-summarizer
skill.description: Calculates basic text statistics, including words, characters, sentences, average word length, and estimated reading time.
skill.summary: Measure text length, sentence count, word length, and reading time.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks and weak cryptography, but those specific detections are false positives in documentation, schema text, and a JavaScript template literal. AI review found a separate high-risk issue: user-controlled text is inserted into an unquoted command template, which may allow command injection if the runner uses shell evaluation.

high_findings.title: Unquoted User Input in Command Template
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill command passes the user-provided text argument directly into a command string without visible quoting or escaping. If the Enact runner evaluates this command through a shell, crafted text could execute unintended commands.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command template contains direct user input interpolation in an executable command. Confidence is below very high because exploitation depends on the runner's command parsing and escaping behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Command Example
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static analyzer flagged fenced usage and example-output blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are Markdown examples and are not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences. It documents usage and output rather than executing commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JavaScript Template Literal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static analyzer flagged an error message template literal as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line formats an error string for JSON output and does not execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are JavaScript string interpolation inside console output. No child process, eval, or shell execution appears at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain descriptions and schema fields for text statistics. No cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only text-analysis metadata and documentation at these locations. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: User Input Interpolated into Executable Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The command template uses direct interpolation for the text input. This pattern should be replaced with safe argument passing or explicit escaping before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The executable command includes the user-controlled text variable. The practical impact depends on how the runtime expands command templates.

content.value_statement: Writers and teams need fast measurements for drafts, notes, and copied text. This skill returns core text statistics for review, planning, and editing.
actual_capabilities: Counts words in a supplied text string.
Counts non-space characters in the text.
Estimates sentence count from punctuation marks.
Estimates reading time at 200 words per minute.
limitations: It does not create an abstractive summary.
Sentence detection is approximate and punctuation based.
It accepts one text input per run.
It does not analyze readability grade or sentiment.

use_cases.title: Check Draft Length
use_cases.description: Measure a blog post or article draft before editing.

use_cases.title: Estimate Reading Time
use_cases.description: Estimate how long users may need to read help text or release notes.

use_cases.title: Validate Short Copy
use_cases.description: Check word and character counts for messages, labels, or summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Basic Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick count for a short passage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text and report the word count, character count, sentence count, average word length, and reading time: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Check a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a longer draft before editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the text statistics skill on this draft. Use the results to identify whether it is too long for a short article: [paste draft]

prompt_templates.title: Compare Copy Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Measure two versions of user-facing text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these two text versions separately and compare their length, sentence count, and estimated reading time: [paste versions]

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Length
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze each section below. Report statistics for each section and identify which sections may need trimming for faster reading: [paste sections]

output_examples.input: Hello world. This is a test.
output_examples.output: The text has 6 words, 22 non-space characters, 2 sentences, an average word length of 3.67, and a 0.03 minute reading time.

output_examples.input: A short release note with one sentence.
output_examples.output: The text has 7 words, 32 non-space characters, 1 sentence, an average word length of 4.57, and a 0.04 minute reading time.
The skill returns word count, character count, estimated sentences, average word length, and estimated reading time.

best_practices: Use plain text input for the most predictable counts.
Review sentence counts manually when text uses abbreviations.
Use reading time as an estimate, not a precise measurement.
anti_patterns: Do not expect it to summarize meaning or key points.
Do not use it as a grammar or style checker.
Do not treat punctuation-based sentence counts as exact.

faq.question: Does this skill create a summary?
faq.answer: No. Despite the name, it calculates text statistics and does not produce a written summary.

faq.question: How is reading time calculated?
faq.answer: It divides the word count by 200 and rounds the result to two decimals.

faq.question: Are spaces included in the character count?
faq.answer: No. The character count excludes whitespace characters.

faq.question: Can it process multiple documents at once?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for one text input per run.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Not yet. The command template should safely handle user input before marketplace publication.
